score_records.title: Independent agent benchmark shows reliability limits in long tool-use chains
score_records.source_authority: Independent evaluation lab with methodology notes and reproducible task definitions.
score_records.executive_takeaway: Agent progress is constrained by reliability across long tool-use chains, not only reasoning benchmark scores.
score_records.why_it_matters: DysonX can use this to separate durable agent capability progress from demos that fail under multi-step workflows.
score_records.watch_next: Watch whether model labs publish recovery mechanisms, benchmark-specific fixes, or eval disclosure changes.
score_records.risk_summary: No critical evidence gap; later confidence and correlation review are still required before publish-readiness work.
score_records.score_explanation: Score 58/65 (89.23%) maps to Tier A: Decision-grade Signal.

score_records.title: Enterprise assistants add governed persistent memory controls
score_records.source_authority: First-party product changelog; useful but not yet independently verified.
score_records.executive_takeaway: Enterprise AI products are shifting persistent memory from feature toggle to governed infrastructure.
score_records.why_it_matters: This helps DysonX track when memory becomes an operational capability layer for agentic products.
score_records.watch_next: Watch for audit logs, retention controls, and cross-tool memory boundaries in regulated deployments.
score_records.risk_summary: Useful but more source support is needed before this can be treated as a durable market shift.
score_records.score_explanation: Score 44/65 (67.69%) maps to Tier B: Useful Signal.

score_records.title: Chip vendor reports lower serving cost for long-context inference
score_records.source_authority: First-party technical note with enough detail for tracking but no independent benchmark yet.
score_records.executive_takeaway: Serving efficiency may be improving, but the Signal is not urgent without an independent workload comparison.
score_records.why_it_matters: Lower serving cost can change which agent workflows are economically viable.
score_records.watch_next: Watch for third-party benchmarks or customer deployment reports using the same long-context workload.
score_records.risk_summary: Useful but not urgent; keep for later review or correlation.
score_records.score_explanation: Score 41/65 (63.08%) maps to Tier B: Useful Signal.

score_records.title: Robotics safety memo may affect embodied-agent deployment claims
score_records.source_authority: Government memorandum summary; primary text is referenced but not sufficiently interpreted.
score_records.executive_takeaway: The item may matter for robotics deployment, but the current analysis does not explain practical capability impact.
score_records.risk_summary: Analysis is thin but source exists; regenerate with clearer capability impact and owner decision value.
score_records.score_explanation: Score 32/65 (49.23%) maps to Tier C: Needs Review.

score_records.title: Several companies say AI demand remains strong
score_records.source_authority: Secondhand market roundup with vague sourcing and no first-source evidence.
score_records.executive_takeaway: This item is too generic to support an Owner decision.
score_records.why_it_matters: It does not yet matter for DysonX because it lacks source authority, AGI capability mapping, and a concrete watch-next point.
score_records.watch_next: Do not track unless a first-source metric or compute deployment claim appears.
score_records.risk_summary: Blocked by generic-summary and weak-attribution risks.
score_records.score_explanation: Score 12/65 (18.46%) is blocked by critical risks.

recommended_next_actions: Resolve records blocked by critical quality risks.
Review SignalQualityScore V1 output against real and fixture audit reports.
Implement Confidence Calibration V1 before publish-readiness decisions.
Implement Multi-source Correlation V1 for high-value or entity-rich signals.
Keep publishing blocked until Human Approval Gate and Publish Readiness Gate exist.
